cashier2: ^An empty cashier's stand with a single fax machine.
hats: ^NOUR: There's no way any of these will fit a normal-sized head.
^NOUR: I... don't want to find out who they're for, actually.
kindle: ^It's a Jungle Kindle filled with e-novels.
^NOUR: ...Looks like most are comics and passionate romance books. Such taste.
^NOUR: Looking closely, I can barely make out some words on the screens...
^NOUR: Impossible to tell what they say, though.
notebooks: ^NOUR: <i>There's nothing like the sight of beautiful blank pages for fresh ideas.<i>
poster1: ^It's a poster of two cats with their tails around each other, and the word "friend" on it.
^NOUR: Historians would say... that they're good friends.
poster2: ^It's a poster with a bald eagle and the word "pride" on it.
^NOUR: Eagles don't evoke much nationalism in anyone...
poster3: ^It's a poster with a cat wearing thick-rimmed glasses.
^NOUR: Poster's right. This is most definitely the pinnacle of "chill".
poster4: ^It's a poster with a fish and the word "swim" on it.
^NOUR: Guess the designer apparently wanted to remind people that fish do, in fact, swim.
shelfwithtrinkets: ^NOUR: They all seem to be familiar shapes, but I... can't tell what they're meant to be.
^NOUR: It looks like some sort of weird... dog?
^NOUR: ... This is giving me a headache.
^NOUR: Since when did B&N sell this stuff, anyway?
shelfwithtrinkets2: ^It's a bunch of trinkets. Shiny, fun-shaped baubles line the shelf.
^NOUR: Can't tell what any of these are supposed to be...
^NOUR: Kind of wish B&N would focus more on selling books again.
volitioncomputer: ^The flesh and wire are one within the Motherboard.
^NOUR: Something feels strangely... sentient about this computer...?
^NOUR: I'm just gonna... stay away from this for now.
bestsellers: ^From crime thriller fiction to award-winning nonfiction, all the bestsellers are here.
^NOUR: ... That is, bestsellers from well over a decade ago.
booksfront: ^There are more books here, which look to be mostly young adult fiction.
^NOUR: ... I'll try not to judge them by their covers.
